behaviors.description: A file created inside a nested subdirectory under the watched root is detected and processed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: in-process event mocks can miss real recursive-watch failures. REAL-INFRA: correctness depends on real filesystem watch behavior for nested paths.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real daemon against temporary input/output directories. Create `input/sub/a.txt` after startup and assert exactly one transformed output file appears for that nested path.

behaviors.description: A second processing request for the same file path inside the dedup window is discarded.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: unit tests on a pure dedup helper can miss wiring/config/clock interactions in the pipeline. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network dependency is required if using local modules with controlled time.
behaviors.test_description: Wire watcher-event intake + dedup store + processor in-process with fake timers. Emit two requests for identical path within the configured window and assert processor runs once; emit again after window and assert second run occurs.

behaviors.description: Multiple OS filesystem events from one write lead to exactly one processing execution.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: duplicate event patterns are OS/watcher-specific; mocked single-event streams can falsely pass.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real filesystem watcher. Perform a write pattern that commonly emits multiple events (e.g., write temp + rename or chunked writes) and assert only one processing/output artifact for the target file.

behaviors.description: If watched directory is deleted and recreated during runtime, daemon reattaches and continues processing without restart.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: watcher invalidation/rebind behavior depends on OS/inode semantics. MOCK-HIDE: synthetic callbacks would miss real detach/recreate failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on real temp directory, process one file successfully, delete watched directory, recreate it at same path, add a new file, and assert it is processed without restarting daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive together, processing order follows file creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering may fail due to real filesystem timestamp precision/ties and event interleaving. Needs real file metadata + watcher timing to catch real regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Create several files in rapid succession in the watched tree, capture their filesystem creation timestamps as observed by daemon logic, and assert processing log/order matches timestamp ordering (with explicit tie-break expectation if equal).

behaviors.description: A file still being written when arrival event fires is deferred until write completion before processing.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: partial-write visibility and event timing are OS/filesystem behaviors; mocks often skip true race conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Open a file for incremental writes while daemon is running, trigger arrival early, verify no processing occurs during active writes, then close/finish write and assert exactly one processing run on the complete content.
